original_abstract: Higher education research is a small field with fuzzy borderlines between those closely attached to this thematic focus and representatives of various disciplines touching this theme occasionally, as well as between researchers and other experts involved in knowledge production on higher education along other tasks. It is a sizeable field of research in some European countries and marginal in others, whereby the themes of research are diverse and the institutional basis is varied. A national focus dominates everywhere. The results of research are unevenly spread across Europe as a consequence of the different role of the English language in the various countries. Europe-wide and international communication and collaboration among higher education researchers has grown substantially since the 1980s, and many analyses are of a comparative nature or address similar developments across countries – as a result of both the researchers' own initiatives and of a growing influence of supra-national policies and developments of higher education. European support for policy-oriented analyses and 'targeted' research is viewed by the researchers both as counter-productive constraints, and as an opportunity for improved research and for a dialogue relevant for the future of higher education research, as well as for the future of higher education policy and practice.
humanized_abstract: Higher education research is a small field with imprecise boundaries between scholars closely affiliated to higher education research and scholars in other disciplines who occasionally engage with higher education research. Also, the boundaries are not always clear-cut between higher education researchers and other experts that are engaged in research on higher education through different channels and activities. Higher education research is a sizeable field of research in some European countries but a marginal one in others. The themes of higher education research are diverse and research is carried out within very different institutional settings in Europe. The national perspective is usually dominant. The distribution of higher education research findings across Europe is very uneven, reflecting the different roles played by the English language in the different European countries. International and European communication and cooperation among higher education researchers have developed strongly since the 1980s. Most studies in the field are comparative studies or are concerned with similar trends and developments in the European countries – either carried out by the researchers' own initiatives or as a consequence of the increasing importance of European-level policy and developments in higher education. The researchers see the European Commission's support to policy-oriented studies and 'targeted' research as either being of restrictive character restricting and obstructive, or as being potentially improving research and facilitating an appropriate dialogue on future higher education research and policy, as well as on the future development of higher education.

The researchers see the European Commission's support to policy-oriented studies and 'targeted' research as either being of restrictive character restricting and obstructive, or as being potentially improving research and facilitating an appropriate dialogue on future higher education research and policy, as well as on the future development of higher education.
